verdict_rationale: All three prior blockers are genuinely closed. BLOCK-1 (secret_service wiring) is resolved — 14a:1689-1695 explicitly wires app.state.scoped_secret_resolver with a 'Do NOT use app.state.secret_service' note; Protocol at 14a:950-955 drops auth_provider_type. BLOCK-2 (affected_nodes producer) is resolved — Task 1 Step 2b mandates the validation.py:1248 co-update with affected_nodes=(identity_finding.node_id,) plus a grep-and-audit-all-call-sites mandate and an integration guard test (test_provenance_row_component_ids_populated_via_real_validate_pipeline). BLOCK-3 (mypy/ruff gates) is resolved — every 14a Task commit block now includes mypy + ruff check + ruff format --check; every 14b Task commit block includes npm run typecheck && npm run lint. Two new warnings require prescription-level fixes before merge: a loading-state stuck-forever bug in the AbortError branch (NW-1) and an incomplete concurrent-fetch test that doesn't exercise the true race (NW-2). Three informational findings follow.

prior_blockers_status.title: secret_service wiring contradiction
prior_blockers_status.evidence: 14a Task 5 Step 4 (lines 1689-1695): wires app.state.scoped_secret_resolver with explicit note at lines 1700-1704 'Do NOT use app.state.secret_service — that is the raw WebSecretService which requires auth_provider_type. Use app.state.scoped_secret_resolver'. Protocol _SecretServiceLike at 14a:950-955 declares list_refs(user_id: str) only — auth_provider_type removed. Unit test _make_service (14a:763-773) uses 'scoped_resolver.list_refs = MagicMock(return_value=list(inventory))' with no auth_provider_type call. Integration test test_secrets_row_uses_scoped_resolver exercises the real call path. Wiring decision is now explicit, not 'adapt to actual attr name'.

prior_blockers_status.title: affected_nodes producer never wired in validation.py
prior_blockers_status.evidence: 14a Task 1 Step 2b (line ~363-390) instructs: in validation.py around line 1248, update the identity_node_advisory ValidationCheck constructor to pass affected_nodes=(identity_finding.node_id,). The plan mandates a grep-and-audit of all ValidationCheck(...) and ValidationError(...) construction sites in the same commit with no compat-shim defaults. Integration guard test test_provenance_row_component_ids_populated_via_real_validate_pipeline (14a:1207-1228) asserts component_ids is non-empty when status == 'warning', catching the case where validation.py is NOT updated but the unit test passes because it supplies affected_nodes manually. Confirmed codebase: schemas.py:50-56 currently shows ValidationCheck without affected_nodes — the plan's co-update is what lands it.

prior_blockers_status.title: mypy/ruff absent from commit checklists
prior_blockers_status.evidence: 14a Task 1 Step 3 commit block: mypy + ruff check + ruff format --check on audit_readiness/ and schemas.py and config.py. Task 2 Step 4: mypy + ruff check + ruff format --check on trust.py. Task 3 Step 3: mypy + ruff check on service.py. Task 4 Step 3: mypy + ruff check on explain.py. Task 5 Step 6: mypy + ruff check on audit_readiness/ and sessions/ownership.py. 14b: npm run typecheck && npm run lint in every Task commit (Tasks 1, 2, 3, 4).

prior_warnings_status.title: Race condition in auditReadinessStore — monotonic write guard + AbortController + concurrent-fetch test
prior_warnings_status.status: PARTIALLY_CLOSED — see NW-1 (loading state) and NW-2 (test gap)
prior_warnings_status.evidence: 14b Task 3 Step 3 (auditReadinessStore.ts) implements: (1) AbortController stored per sessionId; prev.abort() fires before new fetch starts (lines 724-731); (2) monotonic write guard in set() callback (lines 736-742) discards arriving response if cached version is higher; (3) clearSession aborts in-flight controller (lines 783-797). Concurrent-fetch test included (14b:616-643). Guard logic is structurally correct. See NW-1 (loading state bug) and NW-2 (test coverage gap).

prior_warnings_status.title: test_trust.py calls PluginManager().register_builtin_plugins() from tests/unit/
prior_warnings_status.evidence: 14a Task 2 test body (lines 460-509) still calls PluginManager().register_builtin_plugins() inside the test functions. The plan fix-up history (14a:1764-1787) does not mention this warning. Test location unchanged. The tests serve a genuine build-gate function; the concern is classification, not correctness. No regression introduced; warning level unchanged.

prior_warnings_status.title: LookupError catch uses 'from None' — no logging at catch site
prior_warnings_status.evidence: 14a Task 5 Step 3 routes.py (lines 1639-1640): 'raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=str(exc)) from None'. The 'from None' suppresses the chain. Per ELSPETH primacy (audit-first, telemetry-second, logging-last), not adding a log line is correct for a deterministic 404. Matches prior review assessment (REC-3): acceptable because the 404 is deterministic and the frontend surfaces the error state. No change; no regression.

prior_warnings_status.title: Task 7 compareDocumentPosition assertion
prior_warnings_status.evidence: 14c Task 7 test (lines 627-636) still uses panel.compareDocumentPosition(tablist) === Node.DOCUMENT_POSITION_FOLLOWING. Fix-up history (14c:1033-1042) does not mention this warning. Low severity as noted in prior review; the assertion works correctly in jsdom for direct sibling relationships.

new_warnings.title: isLoading not reset in AbortError branch — loading indicator hangs after clearSession
new_warnings.detail: The loadSnapshot catch arm (lines 749-754) handles AbortError with an early return: 'if ((err as { name?: string }).name === 'AbortError') return;'. The early return leaves isLoading at true. clearSession calls ctrl.abort() (line 785) and then calls set() to remove the session's entries from snapshotsBySession, explainsBySession, and abortControllers (lines 787-794), but clearSession's set() does NOT reset isLoading. Result: after clearSession is called for a session with an in-flight request, isLoading remains true indefinitely — the panel shows 'Checking audit readiness...' forever for the next session rendered. Since isLoading is global (not per-session), this affects all subsequent panel renders until a new loadSnapshot completes successfully or the store is reset. Fix: in the AbortError catch branch, also set isLoading: false. Equivalently, add 'isLoading: false' to clearSession's set() payload as a belt-and-suspenders guard.
new_warnings.fix: In the AbortError branch: 'if (...name === 'AbortError') { set({ isLoading: false }); return; }'. Also ensure clearSession's set() resets isLoading: false.
new_warnings.test_gap: The concurrent-fetch test resolves both promises explicitly and does not simulate the clearSession+abort path, so this bug is not caught by the test suite.

new_warnings.title: Concurrent-fetch test is sequential, not concurrent — true race is not exercised
new_warnings.location: auditReadinessStore.test.ts loadSnapshot monotonic guard test (14b lines 616-643)
new_warnings.detail: The test awaits loadSnapshot(SESSION_ID, 2) to full completion before starting loadSnapshot(SESSION_ID, 1). This is a sequential execution, not an overlapping-in-flight race. In a true race, both fetches are in flight simultaneously, and the AbortController-based cancellation is the first line of defense before the monotonic guard. The test only exercises the monotonic guard (correct) but not the abort-based cancellation (which fires when a new loadSnapshot replaces the previous controller mid-flight). The monotonic guard IS correct code and the test does correctly verify it for this sequential pattern. The gap is: if the AbortController abort path has a bug, the test will not catch it because the test never starts two simultaneous in-flight fetches. The fix-up history (14b:1456-1458) claims 'A concurrent-fetch test (fast-v2 / slow-v1 interleaved resolve order) is included' — this is accurate for the monotonic-guard path but the AbortController cancellation path remains untested.
new_warnings.fix: Augment the test to start both fetches concurrently (without awaiting the first), confirm the abort fires by asserting the AbortController's signal.aborted is true, and assert the loading state resets cleanly. The existing sequential test is still useful and should be kept.
new_warnings.risk_level: Low — the monotonic guard catches the visible correctness failure regardless; the abort path is an optimization that cancels the unnecessary network round-trip. Missing abort coverage is a test hygiene issue, not a correctness hole.

informational_findings.title: Task 8.5 guard is a skip-marked test, not a CI-failing gate — framing in review history overstates
informational_findings.detail: 14c Task 8.5 creates subscriptions.handoff.test.ts with describe.skip. The 14c review history (line 1041) states 'Task 8.5 added — Phase 3A handoff guard test that fails red until the relocation lands (mechanical gate replacing prose-only constraint)'. This overstates what is delivered: the test is skipped (not red) while Phase 2 is in flight. The skip-then-unskip pattern is the correct architectural answer given the Phase 2-before-Phase-3A merge sequencing — a truly red CI gate would block Phase 2's merge. The 14c scope notes (lines 816-822) explain the design accurately. The framing mismatch is in the review history prose only, not in the test itself. No code correction needed; the design is correct.
informational_findings.verdict: Acceptable — design is sound; review history prose is imprecise.

informational_findings.title: AbortController cleanup not removed from abortControllers on monotonic-guard discard
informational_findings.location: auditReadinessStore.ts loadSnapshot monotonic-guard set() callback (14b lines 736-747)
informational_findings.detail: When the monotonic guard fires (current cached version > arriving response version), the set() callback returns '{ isLoading: false }' only. The sessionId entry is NOT removed from abortControllers at this point. The AbortController object leaks until either (a) a subsequent loadSnapshot for the same session starts and replaces it via the prev.abort() + new controller path, or (b) clearSession removes it. In practice this is a low-severity leak: AbortControllers are lightweight objects and clearSession always cleans up. No user-visible bug results.
informational_findings.verdict: Informational — no fix required for this review cycle.

informational_findings.title: makeComposition in 14b test fixture uses 'as never' cast for source — NQ9 verified
informational_findings.detail: The makeComposition fixture supplies full NodeSpec required fields (id, node_type, plugin, input, on_success, on_error, options) and uses 'as never' only for the source field (source: { kind: 'csv_file', config: { path: 'x.csv' } } as never). The 14c ReadinessRowDetail.test.tsx makeComposition passes nodes with 'config: {}' and 'as never' cast — this mirrors the real NodeSpec arity at the TypeScript interface boundary (6 required + 6 optional). The 'as never' casts are contained to the source/node fields where TypeScript structural typing requires the test to conform to a union type that the fixture intentionally simplifies. This is a test-ergonomics choice, not a correctness gap — the production code path never handles 'as never' values. Verified: no broader `as never` usage introduced in production code paths.
informational_findings.verdict: Acceptable — test-fixture ergonomics, not production-code concern.

tdd_shape_assessment.detail: All three sub-plans maintain the RED → run-to-fail → GREEN → commit structure. 14a Tasks 1-5 have explicit 'Run → FAIL (ModuleNotFoundError)' / 'Run tests → PASS' steps. 14b Tasks 2-4 have 'Run test — expect FAIL' and 'Run tests — expect PASS' steps with exact vitest commands. 14c Tasks 5-8 follow the same pattern. Test count claims are specific (8/8 for store, 10/10 for panel). The integration guard test (test_provenance_row_component_ids_populated_via_real_validate_pipeline) is the key structural improvement: it tests the real validate_pipeline path, not a MagicMock.

findings.location: auditReadinessStore.ts catch arm: (err as { name?: string }).name === 'AbortError'
findings.assessment: ACCEPTABLE — narrowing an opaque catch(err) at the Tier-3 API boundary (fetch rejection). No defensive .get() or hasattr pattern.

findings.location: auditReadinessStore.ts: apiErr.detail ?? 'Failed to load audit readiness.'
findings.assessment: ACCEPTABLE — trust-boundary coercion for frontend consuming HTTP response (Tier 3). Consistent with prior review assessment.
ACCEPTABLE — compositionState can legitimately be null/undefined in store when no session is active. Optional chaining guards a real nullable state, not a hedge.

findings.location: service.py _build_provenance_row: state_from_record = state_from_record if state_from_record is not None else _default_state_from_record
findings.assessment: ACCEPTABLE — constructor injection with explicit default. The None check is at a protocol boundary (dependency injection), not defensive field access. No hasattr or .get() pattern.

idor_protection.detail: Task 5 Step 3a creates sessions/ownership.py shared verify_session_ownership with 404-not-403 invariant preserved. Both snapshot and explain routes call it. Integration test test_snapshot_404_on_cross_user_access verifies the IDOR path.

xss.detail: No dangerouslySetInnerHTML in any plan code block. Narrative content rendered via <pre> with JSX string interpolation.

production_readiness_summary.error_handling: PRESENT — service raises LookupError; routes catch to 404. Frontend stores render role=alert error states. AbortError handled (with loading-state bug per NW-1).
production_readiness_summary.logging: ABSENT per ELSPETH primacy — correct. Phase 8 telemetry deferred.
production_readiness_summary.configuration: CLEAN — payload_store_retention_days field with documented default.
production_readiness_summary.graceful_degradation: PRESENT — panel returns null for no session or empty composition; error state renders role=alert. Loading indicator on first fetch.
production_readiness_summary.rollback_plan: NOT ADDRESSED — acceptable for UI panel; revert merge and redeploy.

summary.implementation_readiness: APPROVED_WITH_WARNINGS — implement with NW-1 fix applied before merge. NW-2 is a test-coverage gap that should be addressed but does not block merge.
